rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Wertenbroch (2000) as part of the conceptual basis for defining a hedonic-versus-utilitarian consumption continuum in its heterogeneity analysis. This is not just a perfunctory literature list: the distinction is tied to how the authors code redeemed merchandise items and interpret preferences for merchandise versus cash incentives. Although the citation appears in a grouped set, its functional role in construct definition and variable operationalization is clear.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the hedonic-versus-utilitarian goods framework from the cited paper to a sales compensation context, using it to classify reward redemptions and interpret variation in incentive preferences.
